steps.title: Identify the y-intercept
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where the graph crosses the y-axis, which always occurs at $x = 0$.

steps.title: Read the value directly from the graph
steps.content: On the graph, at $x = 0$, the parabola intersects the y-axis at $y = 40$. Therefore, the y-intercept is $(0, 40)$, and the value of $y$ is 40.
